exposes mint edit subcommand over M-EDIT.edit

MP-PKG.NAME: Mint-Python Skeleton
MP-PKG.purpose: Empty parallel package skeleton for the Pure Python Edition (handover §5). Phase 0 ships only `__init__.py` files; subpackage contracts populate in Phases 1-5.
MP-PKG.note: Skeleton only. Contains 8 placeholder subpackages: core, sdk, execution, rules, grace, mcp, cli, tests. No public interfaces in Phase 0.

export-mint_python.PURPOSE: Top-level package marker; __version__ set to 0.0.0 for later bumps

CrossLink.relation: placeholder package; engine flag (MINT_ENGINE / Engine enum) lives in M-CONFIG
placeholder package; engine dispatch chokepoint (_select_engine) lives in M-CLI; future MP-* modules will be routed here

MP-STYLE.NAME: Pure Python Style Primitives
MP-STYLE.purpose: Typography, color, spacing primitives + named style presets for the Pure Python core types

type-Style.PURPOSE: Frozen dataclass: font, size_pt, color_hex, bold, italic, alignment, spacing_before/after, line_height, keep_with_next

fn-Pt.PURPOSE: Pt(value) → int (twentieths of a point); compatible with python-docx units

type-ColorPalette.PURPOSE: Named color resolver tied to a preset (e.g. 'alga_corporate')

fn-load_preset.PURPOSE: Load named preset from registry OR JSON path; validate against schema; return Style namespace (heading1, body, table_header, ...)

MP-CONTENT.NAME: Pure Python Content Primitives
MP-CONTENT.purpose: Inline content blocks placed inside a Section: Paragraph (with optional tab_stops) + Run (with per-run formatting overrides + link/bookmark/footnote anchors) + Image + TabStop primitives. Footnotes bootstrap a /word/footnotes.xml package part on first use; OOXML emit via python-docx + lxml drop-down for w:hyperlink/w:bookmarkStart-End/w:footnoteReference

type-Paragraph.PURPOSE: Styled paragraph with optional tab_stops list; .add_run(text, style=None, *, bold/italic/underline/color/font_size_pt/link/bookmark/footnote) fluent; .render(parent_doc) writes to python-docx Document

type-Run.PURPOSE: Inline styled fragment with optional per-run overrides (bold/italic/underline/color/font_size_pt) + link (external URL or '#anchor') + bookmark (anchor name) + footnote (footnote body text). None = inherit effective Style; explicit value wins

type-Image.PURPOSE: Image embed with .from_path / .from_bytes constructors; .render emits w:drawing with auto rId

MP-TABLE.NAME: Pure Python Table
MP-TABLE.purpose: Table class with §3.3 factory constructors, post-construction shaping, and merged-cells rendering (Cell.colspan/rowspan drive python-docx merge() calls)

type-Cell.PURPOSE: Cell value + optional Style override + colspan/rowspan merge directives (validated >= 1 at construction)

fn-from_list.PURPOSE: Table.from_list(rows) — first row optional header; Cell(value, colspan=N) and Cell(value, rowspan=M) drive merging at render time

fn-financial.PURPOSE: Table.financial(rows) — right-aligned numerics with thousands separator

fn-comparison.PURPOSE: Table.comparison(left, right, rows) — two-column side-by-side preset

MP-SECTION.NAME: Pure Python Section (fluent)
MP-SECTION.purpose: Fluent Section per handover §3.2; blocks union widened to include MP-LIST.List + MP-CALLOUT.Callout; add_list / add_callout available alongside add_paragraph/table/image/chart

type-Section.PURPOSE: Section node holding heading + ordered list of content blocks

fn-add_paragraph.PURPOSE: Append Paragraph or shorthand str; returns self

fn-add_chart.PURPOSE: Append Chart to _blocks; returns self for fluent chaining (Phase-8 unstub commit caf30ab — Section.render walks Chart.render alongside Paragraph/Table/Image)

fn-add_list.PURPOSE: Append List (bullet/numbered/checklist) to _blocks; returns self for fluent chaining

fn-add_callout.PURPOSE: Append Callout (info/warning/code box) to _blocks; returns self for fluent chaining

MP-LIST.NAME: Pure Python List Block (bullet/numbered/checklist)
MP-LIST.purpose: List block — N items rendered as styled python-docx paragraphs (List Bullet / List Number); checklist mode prepends ☐ unicode; level=N applies 0.25*N inch indent

type-List.PURPOSE: List block: items + kind + level; .render(parent_doc) emits one paragraph per item

MP-CALLOUT.NAME: Pure Python Callout Block (info/warning/code)
MP-CALLOUT.purpose: Callout block — visually distinct info / warning / code box rendered as a single-cell python-docx Table with kind-specific shading + thick left accent border + inner cell margins; code kind switches to Courier New monospace
MP-CALLOUT.depends: (none — direct python-docx + lxml drop-down for tcPr/shd/tcBorders/tcMar)

type-Callout.PURPOSE: Callout block: text + kind + optional title; .render(parent_doc) emits a 1x1 table

MP-DOCUMENT.NAME: Pure Python Document Facade
MP-DOCUMENT.purpose: Full §3.1 Document facade; save() produces valid .docx via python-docx + lxml; stubs Phase-3/5 methods
MP-DOCUMENT.depends: MP-SECTION, MP-STYLE, MP-VALIDATE, MP-FIX, MP-GRACE, mint_python._hash

type-Document.PURPOSE: Document facade aggregating Sections, document-wide Style preset, header/footer, cover

type-TOC.PURPOSE: Table-of-contents anchor; rendered as Word TOC field with auto-update

fn-with_style_preset.PURPOSE: Load named preset; returns self for chaining

fn-with_style_from.PURPOSE: Load preset from path; returns self for chaining

fn-add_cover.PURPOSE: Cover page (title + subtitle + optional logo); rendered via python-docx primitives (add_heading + add_paragraph + add_picture + WD_BREAK.PAGE)

fn-save.PURPOSE: Serialize to .docx; emits BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX with section_count + table_count + output_path

fn-inject_grace.PURPOSE: CORE METHOD (delegation pattern): public API on Document per handover §3.1; delegates to MP-GRACE.bootstrap writing GRACE manifest + 10 instruction rules as Custom XML Parts under urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest into the saved .docx, preserving existing customXml entries (Phase-11 unstub commit 3ed23fd)

fn-validate.PURPOSE: Phase-3 (done Wave-9-4): delegates to MP-VALIDATE.run_checks via temp-file save → validate pattern. Returns ValidationReport.

fn-fix.PURPOSE: Phase-3 (done Wave-9-4): delegates to MP-FIX.fix via temp-file save → fix pattern. Returns FixReport.

fn-to_pdf.PURPOSE: CORE METHOD: delegates to Gotenberg HTTP adapter for PDF rendering — saves docx to temp, posts to Gotenberg /forms/libreoffice/convert, writes PDF bytes to the caller-supplied path (Phase-11 unstub commit 4e682d7)

MP-CHART.NAME: Pure Python Chart factories + render
MP-CHART.purpose: Chart class + 7 factory constructors per handover §3.4 + from_matplotlib/seaborn/plotly channels; matplotlib Figure → PNG @ 300 DPI → python-docx add_picture embed; corporate theming + chartjunk removal applied via matplotlib rcParams

type-Chart.PURPOSE: Chart model with cached PNG bytes + chart_type tag + width/height/caption; .render(parent_doc) returns the python-docx inline_shape

fn-line.PURPOSE: Chart.line(labels, values, **kwargs) — line chart with multi-series support

fn-pie.PURPOSE: Chart.pie(labels, values, **kwargs) — pie with corporate palette

fn-heatmap.PURPOSE: Chart.heatmap(matrix, row_labels, col_labels, **kwargs) — matplotlib imshow + colorbar

fn-waterfall.PURPOSE: Chart.waterfall(labels, deltas, **kwargs) — composed from bar primitives

fn-from_matplotlib.PURPOSE: Chart.from_matplotlib(fig, caption=None, width_inches=None, vector=True) — wrap user-built matplotlib Figure

fn-from_seaborn.PURPOSE: Chart.from_seaborn(fig, **kwargs) — lazy-imports seaborn; aliases to from_matplotlib

fn-from_plotly.PURPOSE: Convert plotly Figure to PNG via plotly.io.to_image (kaleido backend, scale=2); lazy-imports plotly — raises ImportError with `pip install plotly kaleido` hint when either dep is missing (Phase-12 unstub commit 7ca3ec3)

MP-SDK.NAME: Pure Python SDK Façade
MP-SDK.purpose: Public re-exports of all §3 types + named-preset registry + with_style_from(path) loader

MP-RULES.NAME: Pure Python OOXML Rule Engine
MP-RULES.purpose: Load OOXML validation rules from shared YAML (rules/*.yaml) and evaluate via lxml XPath. Pure Python successor to M-RULES (src/mint/rules/engine.py).

type-Rule.PURPOSE: Validation rule dataclass: id, format, severity, xpath, check, fix_category, hint, description

type-Violation.PURPOSE: Violation result dataclass: rule_id, severity, fix_category, message, hint, location

type-RuleLoadError.PURPOSE: Raised on broken or missing YAML

fn-evaluate.PURPOSE: Evaluate a single Rule against an lxml-parsed document XML tree; returns Violation or None

fn-all_rules.PURPOSE: Load all YAML rules for a given document format (docx/pptx) from rules_dir

fn-classify_severity.PURPOSE: Return (Severity, FixCategory) tuple for a Violation

fn-get_hint.PURPOSE: Return educational hint dict for a rule_id; fallback for unknown IDs

MP-VALIDATE.NAME: Pure Python Validation Engine
MP-VALIDATE.purpose: Open saved OOXML documents, parse XML via lxml, run all applicable MP-RULES, classify violations by severity+fix_category, produce ValidationReport with mode-based pass/fail. Pure Python successor to M-VALIDATE (src/mint/validate.py).

type-ValidationReport.PURPOSE: Report dataclass: violations, totals by severity, mode string, passed boolean, document_format, filter properties (hard_violations, soft_violations, hard_reject, safe_fixable, visual_fixable, destructive, soft_warn)

type-SeverityMode.PURPOSE: Self-defined StrEnum: AUDIT, LENIENT, STRICT — decoupled from mint.config.SeverityMode

type-InvalidDocumentError.PURPOSE: Raised by _open_document_xml on file/ZIP/XML errors; caught by run_checks → XML-001 pseudo-rule report

fn-run_checks.PURPOSE: Open docx/pptx, parse XML, evaluate all rules, classify, return ValidationReport. Catches InvalidDocumentError → XML-001 report (never raises at top level)

fn-validate.PURPOSE: Legacy-compatible alias: calls run_checks(doc_path, severity_mode, rules_dir)

fn-_detect_format.PURPOSE: Detect format from file extension: .docx→docx, .pptx→pptx, else InvalidDocumentError

fn-_get_main_xml_path.PURPOSE: Map format to ZIP entry path: docx→word/document.xml, pptx→ppt/slides/slide1.xml

fn-_open_document_xml.PURPOSE: Open ZIP, read main XML, parse via lxml → (tree, format); raises InvalidDocumentError on any failure

fn-classify_violations.PURPOSE: Group violations by severity+fix_category → dict; empty list → empty groups

MP-FIX.NAME: Pure Python Auto-Fix Engine
MP-FIX.purpose: Apply safe/visual auto-fixes to OOXML documents with backup, cascade detection (max 3 iterations), destructive rejection. Pure Python successor to M-FIX (src/mint/fix.py). D-H09 newline fix only in Phase-9 scope.
MP-FIX.depends: MP-VALIDATE (run_checks, SeverityMode, ValidationReport, InvalidDocumentError), MP-RULES (Violation, FixCategory, Severity), mint_python._hash, zipfile, lxml, shutil, io

type-FixReport.PURPOSE: Report dataclass: fixed_path, backup_path, iterations, applied_fixes, remaining_violations, diff summary

type-CascadeDetectedError.PURPOSE: Raised when fixes oscillate without converging within max_iterations

type-DestructiveRejectedError.PURPOSE: Raised when fix attempt includes DESTRUCTIVE-category violations

fn-create_backup.PURPOSE: Create .docx.bak with identical SHA256 content; raises BackupFailedError on failure

fn-apply_fixes.PURPOSE: Filter destructive → backup → iterate safe/visual fixes → re-validate → cascade check → return FixReport

fn-fix.PURPOSE: Convenience: calls run_checks → apply_fixes → returns FixReport

fn-_apply_simple_fix.PURPOSE: Apply D-H09 newline fix only (w:t \n → ); returns False for all other rule_ids (no fix logic in Phase-9 scope)

CrossLink.relation: Paragraph and Run consume Style for typography/color/spacing
Table.apply_style and the financial/comparison factories consume Style
Table.apply_style and the financial/comparison factories consume Style
Section.add_paragraph / add_image consume Paragraph and Image
Section.add_table consumes Table
Document body is an ordered list of Sections
Document.with_style_preset registers a Style namespace inherited by descendants
SDK re-exports Document and TOC
SDK re-exports Table
SDK re-exports Image
SDK re-exports Style, Pt, ColorPalette + hosts the preset registry
Chart factories pull active Style preset to apply matplotlib rcParams (font, color cycle, chartjunk removal)
Section.add_chart(chart) appends to _blocks (Phase-8 unstubs the BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD path); Section.render walks Chart.render alongside Paragraph/Table/Image
Section.add_list(list_block) appends to _blocks; Section.render walks List.render alongside other block types
Section.add_callout(callout) appends to _blocks; Section.render walks Callout.render alongside other block types
SDK re-exports Chart per handover §3.4 surface list (Phase-8 addition)
SDK re-exports List + ListKind
SDK re-exports Callout + CalloutKind
Document.validate() delegates to MP-VALIDATE.run_checks via temp-file pattern (Wave-9-4 unstub)
Document.fix() delegates to MP-FIX.fix via temp-file pattern (Wave-9-4 unstub)

MP-GRACE.NAME: Pure Python GRACE Injector v1.1
MP-GRACE.purpose: Pure Python successor to M-GRACE: emit GRACE manifest + instructions as Custom XML Parts under urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest into a saved .docx, preserving any existing Custom XML Parts from other vendors. Called via delegation from MP-DOCUMENT.inject_grace; deps: zipfile + xml.etree + tempfile + uuid + mint_python._hash.
MP-GRACE.depends: zipfile, xml.etree.ElementTree, mint_python._hash, uuid, tempfile, logging (stdlib)

type-GRACEManifest.PURPOSE: Manifest dataclass: document_structure dict, fingerprint hash, instructions list, namespace, xml_part_name

type-GRACEInjectionError.PURPOSE: Raised on path-traversal in source ZIP, missing source file, or structure analysis failure

export-GRACE_INSTRUCTIONS.PURPOSE: Default 10-rule instruction list bootstrapped into the manifest when caller does not supply explicit rules

fn-bootstrap.PURPOSE: Inject GRACE manifest + instructions Custom XML Part into source .docx; computes fingerprint sha256 of source; emits [MP-Grace][inject][BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST] at INFO with part_name + fingerprint[:16]; returns GRACEManifest

fn-describe.PURPOSE: Read existing GRACE manifest from .docx if present (looks for grace/*.xml inside the ZIP); returns GRACEManifest or None

MP-HASH.NAME: Shared SHA-256 File Hasher
MP-HASH.purpose: Internal utility: SHA-256 file hash with 64 KB chunked reading. Shared by MP-FIX and MP-GRACE.

fn-compute_file_hash.PURPOSE: Return SHA-256 hex digest of file at path

CrossLink.relation: Document.inject_grace is a delegation chokepoint: the public API lives on Document per handover §3.1; MP-GRACE.bootstrap writes the urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest Custom XML Parts into the saved .docx (Phase-11 unstub).

MP-MD-ADAPTER.NAME: Markdown → ArticleSpec walker
MP-MD-ADAPTER.purpose: Deterministic walker — markdown text in, ArticleSpec dataclass out. Uses markdown-it-py 4.2 with the gfm-like preset to parse the source into a token tree, then walks the tree producing typed blocks (paragraph / callout / list / table / code) and SpecSections. No LLM. Exists because LLM-driven structuring of dense markdown (especially tables) is unreliable on non-frontier models — confirmed empirically with the 0.4.0a1 baseline_report regression. Markdown is structured input; structure should be extracted by code.
MP-MD-ADAPTER.depends: markdown-it-py (4.2, new dep), tools/article_experiment/spec (ArticleSpec + block dataclasses, types reused)

fn-markdown_to_spec.PURPOSE: Convert markdown text to ArticleSpec via markdown-it-py walker; deterministic; emits [MP-MdAdapter][parse][BLOCK_PARSE_MD] at INFO with token / section / block counts

type-MarkdownAdapterError.PURPOSE: Base error for adapter-level failures (empty input, no usable content)

MP-MCP-AUDIT.NAME: MCP server inventory + migration plan (design-only)
MP-MCP-AUDIT.purpose: Design-only deliverable: read existing src/mint/mcp_g1.py and src/mint/mcp_g2.py, inventory all FastMCP @mcp.tool registrations, classify each (stateful/stateless, elicitation-aware/one-shot, kept/migrate/deprecate), perform gap analysis vs the new product shape (design-tokens-as-resources, versioned templates, GRACE-as-audit-trail, elicitation-driven planning), produce a migration plan. Output is docs/mcp-audit.md as a human-readable architecture review — NO runtime code in this module. The doc becomes the brief for Phase-14 (actual migration). Audit-only scope is deliberate: keeps research separate from engineering.
MP-MCP-AUDIT.depends: src/mint/mcp_g1.py (read), src/mint/mcp_g2.py (read), src/mint/llm.py (read for client patterns)

doc-mcp-audit.PURPOSE: Sectioned report — inventory, state analysis, gap analysis, migration plan, new-tool roadmap. Reviewed by user as the verification mechanism.

MP-MEMO-POC.NAME: Memo MCP tool with FastMCP elicitation (planning-mode wow-demo)
MP-MEMO-POC.purpose: End-to-end POC of the planning-mode dialog pattern via the MCP elicitation primitive (spec 2025-06-18, FastMCP Context.elicit). Single tool create_memo(intent, source_md?, ctx) parses intent + optional markdown source via MP-MD-ADAPTER, populates a partial MemoSpec (5 required fields: sender / recipient / date / subject / body), elicits each missing field through ctx.elicit (the connected MCP client renders a structured form to the user — Claude Desktop primary, OpenWebUI / Cursor / Copilot follow), assembles the docx with the klawd preset and a memo-specific YAML template (templates/memo.yaml), injects a GRACE audit-trail manifest, returns path + audit_id + fields_elicited. Memo template is a YAML data file (per the "design as data, not code" rule) — sets the precedent for future doc types (Letter, Report, Contract).
MP-MEMO-POC.depends: MP-MD-ADAPTER, MP-DOCUMENT, MP-STYLE, MP-GRACE, fastmcp.Context (elicit), pyyaml

fn-create_memo.PURPOSE: @mcp.tool async function. Parses intent + optional source_md, fills MemoSpec heuristically, elicits each missing required field via ctx.elicit, builds the docx with klawd preset + memo template, injects GRACE manifest, returns {path, audit_id, fields_elicited}. Emits [MP-Memo][create][BLOCK_PARSE_INTENT], [MP-Memo][elicit][BLOCK_ELICIT_FIELD] per field, [MP-Memo][build][BLOCK_BUILD_DOCX], [MP-Memo][grace][BLOCK_INJECT_GRACE]

type-MemoSpec.PURPOSE: Frozen dataclass with 5 required POC fields: sender, recipient, date, subject, body. Extends in Phase-14+ with cc, signature, classification, distribution_list

type-MemoTemplate.PURPOSE: Loader for templates/memo.yaml — declares field order, layout (header / body / signature blocks), klawd-derived styling refinements

type-MemoElicitationRejected.PURPOSE: MEMO_ELICITATION_REJECTED — raised when the user declines to provide a required field via the MCP client

CrossLink.relation: markdown_to_spec produces an ArticleSpec consumed by build_document_from_spec via the standard MP-DOCUMENT save pipeline (DF-009). Closes the loop md → docx without any LLM in the production path.

create_memo calls markdown_to_spec on the optional source_md to extract structured content for fact-finding (subject / date / body candidates) before elicitation kicks in for whatever's still missing.

create_memo assembles the final docx through MP-DOCUMENT — the MemoTemplate maps MemoSpec fields onto Document.add_section / set_header calls; klawd preset is applied via apply_preset_to_doc on Document.save (DF-010 step 5).
create_memo applies the klawd preset (apply_preset_to_doc) so generated memos visually match the Anthropic baseline. Verified by V-MP-MEMO-POC scenario-6: styles.xml carries 1B3A5C / Arial / 333333.

create_memo injects a GRACE manifest (urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest) carrying audit-trail metadata (audit_id, fields_elicited, model identity, timestamp). Establishes the GRACE-as-audit-trail differentiator end-to-end.

MP-MCP-AUDIT's roadmap section uses MP-MEMO-POC.create_memo as the canonical example of the new tool signature (async, ctx: Context, ctx.elicit-based dialog). Phase-14 migration of legacy mcp_g1 / mcp_g2 tools follows the same shape.

MP-DOC-GENERIC.NAME: Generic create_document MCP tool
MP-DOC-GENERIC.purpose: Lift Phase-13 MEMO-POC out of the hardcoded "memo" shape into a generic create_document(intent, doc_type, source_md?, ctx) tool. Document type selected at call-time from templates/ registry; pipeline (heuristic → elicit → klawd → GRACE) identical for every doc_type. Adding Letter / Report / Contract becomes a templates/<name>.yaml file with no Python changes.
MP-DOC-GENERIC.depends: MP-MEMO-POC (refactor source), MP-MD-ADAPTER, MP-DOCUMENT, MP-STYLE, MP-GRACE, MP-TEMPLATES-REGISTRY, fastmcp.Context, pyyaml

fn-create_document.PURPOSE: @server.tool async; doc_type selects template; pipeline doc-type-agnostic

type-DocumentSpec.PURPOSE: Generic dataclass driven by template required_fields; replaces MemoSpec

MP-TEMPLATES-REGISTRY.NAME: Template registry + read MCP tools
MP-TEMPLATES-REGISTRY.purpose: Read-only registry over templates/ — discovers YAML files (and semver-versioned siblings), validates against TemplateSchema, exposes them through list_templates / get_template MCP tools. First half of audit's Priority-2.

type-TemplateRegistry.PURPOSE: Walks templates/, parses + validates YAML files; returns list/get queries

fn-get_template.PURPOSE: @server.tool async; returns full YAML content for name+version

type-TemplateSchema.PURPOSE: Generic schema replacing MemoTemplate; required_fields + layout + doc_type + version + description

MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE.NAME: Template versioning + cross-model handoff
MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE.purpose: Versioned write side of the template registry. update_template authors a new semver-bumped YAML file; never overwrites. Sidecar audit log records every write. GRACE manifest in produced docx records template lineage. Closes audit's Priority-4 — frontier model edits in low-sensitivity context, local model later consumes on sensitive data, manifest names the chain.
MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE.depends: MP-TEMPLATES-REGISTRY (validation + version detection), MP-GRACE (manifest extension carries template lineage), pyyaml

fn-update_template.PURPOSE: @server.tool async; bumps semver + writes new file + appends audit-log entry

type-TemplateAuditEntry.PURPOSE: Sidecar JSONL record per write: timestamp, name, version, predecessor_version, author, content_sha256

MP-MCP-RESOURCES.NAME: MCP resources for templates + presets
MP-MCP-RESOURCES.purpose: FastMCP @server.resource handlers exposing mint://template/<name> and mint://preset/<name> URI schemes. Native enumeration for clients that prefer resources/list (Cursor, VS Code Insiders MCP). Symmetric tools list_presets / get_preset for clients without resource support (current Claude Desktop). Closes audit's Priority-1.

export-resource_handlers.PURPOSE: @server.resource handlers for mint://template/* and mint://preset/* URIs

fn-get_preset.PURPOSE: @server.tool async; returns full YAML/JSON preset content

CrossLink.relation: MP-DOC-GENERIC supersedes MP-MEMO-POC's hardcoded memo flow; pipeline (heuristic → elicit → klawd → GRACE) is inherited verbatim, only specialization moves out into templates/.
create_document looks up doc_type in the registry to fetch the template; registry validates schema before pipeline runs.
Inherited from MEMO-POC: source_md is parsed via markdown_to_spec for fact extraction.
Inherited: docx assembly through MP-DOCUMENT with klawd preset application.
GRACE manifest extended with doc_type + template_version + template_author for full lineage.
Write side validates content against the registry's TemplateSchema before persisting; new versions land in the same directory the registry walks.
Audit log entries flow into GRACE manifest of every produced docx — establishing the cross-model handoff lineage chain.
Resource handler for mint://template/* delegates to the registry's get_template; resource enumeration mirrors list_templates.
Resource handler for mint://preset/* reads from MP-STYLE.BUILTIN_PRESETS.

MP-AUTH-SHIM.NAME: Authorization shim for write-path MCP tools
MP-AUTH-SHIM.purpose: Production-blocker: gate update_template (and any future write tools) behind a config-driven author allowlist. MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS env or ~/.config/mint/writers.json define the allowlist; empty config = open mode with one-shot warning. Whitespace-only env value also falls through to open mode (deployment safety: a misconfigured env doesn't lock everyone out). Read tools stay open. Closes audit Priority-4's authorization-shim sub-clause that was left as TODO at Phase-14 close.
MP-AUTH-SHIM.depends: stdlib only (os, json, pathlib, dataclasses, logging)
MP-AUTH-SHIM.consumed-by: MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE (TemplateRegistry.update calls require_template_writer immediately after the empty-author guard, before any disk I/O)

fn-is_template_writer.PURPOSE: pure bool check on (author, config); no side effects

fn-require_template_writer.PURPOSE: raises TemplateWriteForbidden on deny; admit path silent in open mode (only BLOCK_AUTH_OPEN_MODE on first call); admit emits BLOCK_AUTH_ADMIT in env/file mode

fn-load_writers_config.PURPOSE: env > file > none precedence; cached per-process; cache-poisons on AuthConfigInvalid (re-raises from cache); emits BLOCK_AUTH_OPEN_MODE WARNING once when empty

fn-_reset_for_tests.PURPOSE: test-only cache reset; consumed by tests/unit/conftest.py::clean_writers_config fixture

type-WritersConfig.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass: writers tuple[str, ...], source Literal['env','file','none'], open_mode bool

type-TemplateWriteForbidden.PURPOSE: exception raised when author is not on the allowlist; carries author + config_source

type-AuthConfigInvalid.PURPOSE: exception raised at first call when writers.json is malformed; cache-poisoned for the rest of the process

log-marker-BLOCK_AUTH_OPEN_MODE.PURPOSE: WARNING; once per process when config resolves to open mode; subsequent admits silent

log-marker-BLOCK_AUTH_ADMIT.PURPOSE: INFO; payload author=<id> config_source=<env|file>; not emitted on open-mode admits

log-marker-BLOCK_AUTH_DENY.PURPOSE: INFO; payload author=<id> reason=not_in_writers config_source=<env|file>; full writers tuple never logged (forbidden-3)

MP-MANIFEST-READ.NAME: GRACE manifest read tool (mint_read_grace_manifest)
MP-MANIFEST-READ.purpose: Symmetric read counterpart to GRACE injection. mint_read_grace_manifest opens any docx, locates grace/manifest_*.xml, and returns the parsed manifest as a canonical dict (10 keys; missing values surface as None for forward-compat). Most-recent-wins selection across multi-manifest docx (parsed timestamp, NOT namelist order). Closes audit Priority-1 acceptance criterion "read_grace_manifest exists and round-trips a manifest written by any other tool" — explicitly named in audit §1 row-7 + §3(c) + §5 roadmap, but never shipped during Phase-14.
MP-MANIFEST-READ.path: src/mint_python/mcp/manifest.py + module-tail deferred-import in src/mint_python/mcp/document.py to register @server.tool
MP-MANIFEST-READ.depends: MP-GRACE (existing fn-describe + _parse_manifest_xml), mint._security.safe_doc (raises ValueError on traversal; tool re-raises as InvalidDocument), fastmcp.Context, fastmcp.exceptions.ToolError
MP-MANIFEST-READ.consumed-by: External MCP clients via mint_read_grace_manifest tool; future audit-flow consumers of the canonical 10-key dict

fn-mint_read_grace_manifest.PURPOSE: @server.tool async; wraps MP-GRACE.describe; canonicalizes to 10-key dict; surfaces ManifestNotFound / ManifestParseError / InvalidDocument as structured tool errors (no traceback to client)

fn-_canonicalize.PURPOSE: GRACEManifest dataclass + _audit_instructions list → canonical 10-key dict; parses key=value strings from instructions list into typed fields; missing keys surface as None for forward-compat

fn-_select_most_recent.PURPOSE: across N grace/manifest_*.xml parts, returns the parsed manifest with highest timestamp; tie-break on missing-timestamp deterministic

fn-_read_all_manifests.PURPOSE: enumerates grace/manifest_*.xml entries in the docx zip; opens with mode='r' explicitly; returns list of (xml_part_name, parsed_manifest) tuples

type-ManifestNotFound.PURPOSE: structured tool error: docx has no urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest custom XML part

type-ManifestParseError.PURPOSE: structured tool error: manifest XML present but unparseable; carries xml_part_name + document_path

type-InvalidDocument.PURPOSE: structured tool error: not a valid .docx zip OR path traversal rejected by safe_doc

const-CANONICAL_KEYS.PURPOSE: tuple of the 10 keys always present in returned dict (audit_id, model_identity, fields_elicited, template, template_version, template_author, timestamp, instructions, fingerprint, namespace)

const-GRACE_NAMESPACE.PURPOSE: urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest — always returned as the namespace value

log-marker-BLOCK_READ_MANIFEST.PURPOSE: INFO; payload document_path manifest_count selected_xml_part_name; full dict NEVER in INFO records (forbidden-3)

MP-VISUAL-QA-HOOK.NAME: Visual QA hook on create_document (VLM rubric)
MP-VISUAL-QA-HOOK.purpose: Advisory post-create_document visual quality gate. Refactors root-level e2e_qa_visual.py into a library; runs after every successful create_document. Score 0-100 across typography / layout / visual_richness / professional axes via qwen2.5vl:7b on Ollama. Default ON; opt out via MINT_SKIP_VISUAL_QA=1. NEVER fails the tool — two-layer defense in depth (score_document outer except + caller try/except wrap). VF-019 inv-7 NO-CALLER-INPUT-IN-VLM-PAYLOAD: rubric prompt is FIXED with NO doc_title interpolation (the original e2e_qa_visual.py used core.xml dc:title which derived from the caller's intent — removed in Wave-15-2 refactor).
MP-VISUAL-QA-HOOK.path: src/mint_python/qa/__init__.py + src/mint_python/qa/visual.py + edits to src/mint_python/mcp/document.py (hook insertion between END_BLOCK_INJECT_GRACE and the return dict) + e2e_qa_visual.py rewritten as 56-LOC argparse CLI shim delegating to score_document
MP-VISUAL-QA-HOOK.depends: soffice (LibreOffice headless), pdftoppm (poppler-utils), Ollama VLM endpoint qwen2.5vl:7b (configurable via MINT_VISUAL_QA_OLLAMA_URL + MINT_VISUAL_QA_VLM_MODEL ops overrides), urllib.request stdlib; integrated into MP-DOC-GENERIC.create_document via post-step
MP-VISUAL-QA-HOOK.consumed-by: MP-DOC-GENERIC._run_pipeline (hook caller); e2e_qa_visual.py CLI shim (manual smoke)

fn-score_document.PURPOSE: library function returning VisualQAReport | None; None when MINT_SKIP_VISUAL_QA=1 (caller opt-out); VisualQAReport(skipped=True) when backend/network/decode failure (key present so ops sees signal); never raises back into the tool (outer except Exception maps any uncaught raise to skipped=True with skip_reason='unexpected:<ExcClass>')

type-VisualQAReport.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass: score float, axes dict[str, int], issues list[str], pages_scored int, preset_name str, advisory bool=True, skipped bool=False, skip_reason str | None

env-MINT_SKIP_VISUAL_QA.PURPOSE: when '1', hook short-circuits BEFORE backend probes; score_document returns None; caller omits visual_qa key entirely from structured_content (key absent, not None)

env-MINT_VISUAL_QA_OLLAMA_URL.PURPOSE: ops override for Ollama endpoint URL; not caller-controllable

env-MINT_VISUAL_QA_VLM_MODEL.PURPOSE: ops override for VLM model name; not caller-controllable

log-marker-BLOCK_QA_SCORE.PURPOSE: INFO; payload preset pages_scored score axes; emitted on success path AFTER BLOCK_INJECT_GRACE in caplog order

log-marker-BLOCK_QA_BELOW_THRESHOLD.PURPOSE: WARNING; payload score threshold preset; emitted when aggregate < threshold (default 70); tool still succeeds

log-marker-BLOCK_QA_BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE.PURPOSE: WARNING; payload reason=<backend>_unavailable | render_failed:<Exc> | no_pages_rendered | ollama_unreachable:<Exc>; emitted on graceful-fail paths

const-_PROMPT_TEMPLATE.PURPOSE: FIXED rubric prompt; parameterized only by page_no + total_pages (numeric); NO caller-derived content (intent / source_md / fields_elicited / doc_title) per VF-019 inv-7

MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION.NAME: Remove mcp_g1/mcp_g2 + JS sandbox + legacy create/assemble pipeline
MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION.purpose: Subtractive cleanup honoring audit §6 "Tools to deprecate" + the unmet Phase-14 promise. Pre-flight 2026-05-10 expanded scope: also deletes mint.create + mint.assemble (legacy JS-tier orchestrators superseded by mint_python.mcp.document.create_document since Phase-14) and the cmd_serve CLI subcommand mount path. Drops Engine.JS, purges docx/pptxgenjs/exceljs/vm2/isolated-vm/vitest/eslint from pyproject + uv.lock, updates docs/technology.xml + docs/requirements.xml. Pure-python becomes the only execution surface. Net public surface SHRINKS by 5 legacy MCP tools + 2 legacy orchestration modules + 1 CLI subcommand.
MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION.path: src/mint/mcp_g1.py (delete) + src/mint/mcp_g2.py (delete) + src/mint/sandbox/ (delete recursive) + src/mint/create.py (delete; scope-expanded) + src/mint/assemble.py (delete; scope-expanded) + src/mint/cli.py (edit: drop cmd_serve + Engine.JS) + src/mint/config.py (drop Engine.JS) + pyproject.toml (drop deps + dangling entry points) + docs/technology.xml + docs/requirements.xml
MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION.depends: Pre-flight audit complete 2026-05-10: tools/article_experiment/ + scripts/ clean. In-tree importers (mint.create, mint.assemble, mint.cli) all in deletion scope — no stub-shim needed.

removal-mcp_g1.PURPOSE: DELETE src/mint/mcp_g1.py — successors are mint_python.mcp.* tools

removal-mcp_g2.PURPOSE: DELETE src/mint/mcp_g2.py — superseded by mint_create_document + mint_python list_templates / mint_edit (TBD)

removal-sandbox.PURPOSE: DELETE src/mint/sandbox/ — Node.js vm2/isolated-vm sandbox, dead code under pure-python runtime

removal-create.PURPOSE: DELETE src/mint/create.py — legacy M-CREATE orchestrator (~120 LOC); imports mint.sandbox; superseded by mint_python.mcp.document.create_document since Phase-14. Scope-expansion 2026-05-10.

removal-assemble.PURPOSE: DELETE src/mint/assemble.py — legacy M-ASSEMBLE modular generator (~700 LOC); only consumer was mint.create. Scope-expansion 2026-05-10.

removal-cmd_serve.PURPOSE: REMOVE cmd_serve subcommand from src/mint/cli.py — legacy MCP server mount path (FastMCP('MINT').mount(g1).mount(g2)). MCP entry point becomes src/mint_python/mcp/document.py invoked via stdio. Scope-expansion 2026-05-10.

removal-engine_js.PURPOSE: REMOVE Engine.JS branch from src/mint/cli.py::_select_engine; --engine js arg removed or stubbed to point at pure-python successors

removal-deps.PURPOSE: REMOVE docx, pptxgenjs, exceljs, vm2, isolated-vm, vitest, eslint from pyproject.toml + uv.lock; update docs/technology.xml Runtime/Deps/Tooling + docs/requirements.xml constraint-7

CrossLink.relation: MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE.update_template calls require_template_writer(author) BEFORE any disk write. Empty config = open mode (warns once); explicit allowlist = TEMPLATE_WRITE_FORBIDDEN on miss. Closes audit Priority-4 sub-clause.
mint_read_grace_manifest is a thin MCP wrapper over MP-GRACE.describe — read counterpart to MP-GRACE.bootstrap. No re-implementation; canonicalizes dict shape and surfaces tool-level errors.
Round-trip evidence chain: docx produced by create_document carries a manifest; mint_read_grace_manifest must return the same audit_id / fields_elicited / template_version / template_author back.
Hook integrated as a post-step in create_document, AFTER MP-GRACE.bootstrap. Attaches visual_qa to structured_content as advisory; never blocks document return.
score_document accepts preset_name (currently always 'klawd' via the create_document hook). Future presets parameterize the rubric or the reference comparison.

The deprecation removes mint_create / mint_extract_style / mint_list_templates legacy registrations; mint_create_document + mint_python list_templates + (TBD) mint_edit successors carry the surface forward.

Legacy mint_create stripped GRACE injection altogether; pure-python create_document + mint_read_grace_manifest are the only paths after Phase-15. The 'GRACE-as-audit-trail story is now enforced' line in audit §3(c) finally becomes literally true.

MP-MCP-VALIDATE.NAME: mint_validate_document MCP tool (A1)
MP-MCP-VALIDATE.purpose: Closes the MCP-parity gap for the validation surface. Thin wrap around mint_python.validate.validate; read-only; no allowlist (V-MP-AUTH-SHIM forbidden-1 extends to read tools). Returns a canonical 5-key ValidationReport dict (stable key order).
MP-MCP-VALIDATE.path: src/mint_python/mcp/validate.py + module-tail deferred-import in src/mint_python/mcp/document.py to register @server.tool
MP-MCP-VALIDATE.consumed-by: External MCP clients via mint_validate_document tool

fn-mint_validate_document.PURPOSE: @server.tool async; wraps mint_python.validate.validate; canonicalizes ValidationReport dataclass to 5-key dict; surfaces structured errors with no traceback bleed

fn-_canonicalize_report.PURPOSE: ValidationReport dataclass → 5-key dict (passed, severity_mode, violations, counts, format) with stable key order

fn-_canonicalize_violation.PURPOSE: Violation dataclass → 5-key sub-dict (rule_id, severity, message, hint, location)

fn-_resolve_severity_mode.PURPOSE: Literal['audit','lenient','strict'] → SeverityMode; case-insensitive; raises InvalidDocument on unknown values

const-CANONICAL_VALIDATE_KEYS.PURPOSE: frozenset of dict keys always present in returned report (5 keys) — VF-020 inv-5 STABLE-KEYS oracle

type-InvalidDocument.PURPOSE: fastmcp.exceptions.ToolError subclass; structured error for path-traversal / not-zip / unknown severity_mode

type-ValidationBackendError.PURPOSE: fastmcp.exceptions.ToolError subclass; structured error for rules-dir missing / lxml parse failure

log-marker-BLOCK_VALIDATE_DONE.PURPOSE: INFO; payload severity_mode hard_count soft_count passed

MP-MCP-FIX.NAME: mint_fix_document MCP tool (A2)
MP-MCP-FIX.purpose: Closes the MCP-parity gap for the auto-fix surface. Thin wrap calling mint_python.fix.run_checks + mint_python.fix.apply_fixes directly (NOT the high-level fix() helper) so the max_iterations contract parameter is honored. Mutates caller-supplied .docx in place; preserves the backup-before-write invariant. No allowlist — caller-owned doc, not template/preset.
MP-MCP-FIX.path: src/mint_python/mcp/fix.py + module-tail deferred-import in src/mint_python/mcp/document.py to register @server.tool
MP-MCP-FIX.depends: mint_python.fix.apply_fixes + FixReport + error types (DestructiveRejectedError, BackupFailedError, CascadeDetectedError), mint_python.validate.run_checks + SeverityMode (re-validation), mint._security.safe_doc, fastmcp.Context + ToolError
MP-MCP-FIX.consumed-by: External MCP clients via mint_fix_document tool

fn-mint_fix_document.PURPOSE: @server.tool async; orchestrates run_checks → apply_fixes (with max_iterations cap) → canonicalize; surfaces DESTRUCTIVE_REJECTED / BACKUP_FAILED / CASCADE_DETECTED / INVALID_DOCUMENT as structured errors

fn-_canonicalize_report.PURPOSE: FixReport dataclass → 6-key dict (applied_fixes, remaining_violations, backup_path, iterations, diff_summary, severity_mode); fixed_path omitted (caller already knows it)

type-BackupFailed.PURPOSE: can't write .bak (permission / disk); raised before in-place mutation

log-marker-BLOCK_FIX_DONE.PURPOSE: INFO; payload iterations applied_count remaining_violations backup_path

log-marker-BLOCK_DESTRUCTIVE_REJECTED.PURPOSE: INFO; deny path; payload violation count

MP-FINGERPRINT.NAME: Pure-Python fingerprint port (A3 port)
MP-FINGERPRINT.purpose: Port of src/mint/fingerprint.py to the pure-python tree. Preserves the legacy public surface names (fingerprint / compute / compare / FingerprintResult / DriftStatus / FingerprintError / MissingStyleXmlError / HashFailedError) but RESHAPES per Phase-16 dev-plan output spec: FingerprintResult fields are {hash, format, has_styles_xml, byte_count} (legacy had {hash, document_path, format, xml_sources}); DriftStatus.UNKNOWN replaces legacy BASELINE_MISSING. Hash value byte-identical to legacy (porting-parity scenario-5 holds). Legacy module stays in place additively. Constraint-8 compliant.
MP-FINGERPRINT.depends: stdlib only (hashlib, zipfile, pathlib, dataclasses, enum, logging). Constraint-8 compliant — 0 hits on `from mint\.|import mint\.` grep.
MP-FINGERPRINT.consumed-by: MP-MCP-FINGERPRINT (Phase-16 W2; wraps fingerprint + compare); future drift-detection callers under mint_python/

fn-fingerprint.PURPOSE: public entry; thin wrapper over compute()

fn-compute.PURPOSE: opens docx zip, locates styles.xml, SHA-256 over chunked content (_CHUNK_SIZE = 64 KB); raises MissingStyleXmlError / HashFailedError

fn-compare.PURPOSE: pure equality check; None inputs → DriftStatus.UNKNOWN

fn-_hash_member.PURPOSE: private; chunk-streamed update of a sha256() over one zip member; returns (chunks_read, byte_count)

type-FingerprintResult.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass: hash str, format str, has_styles_xml bool, byte_count int (RESHAPED from legacy {hash,document_path,format,xml_sources})

type-DriftStatus.PURPOSE: StrEnum {MATCH, DRIFT, UNKNOWN} (UNKNOWN replaces legacy BASELINE_MISSING)

type-MissingStyleXmlError.PURPOSE: exception; raised when docx lacks word/styles.xml

type-HashFailedError.PURPOSE: exception; raised on I/O / hash-backend failure mid-read

const-_CHUNK_SIZE.PURPOSE: 64 KB chunk size for streamed SHA-256; hash output byte-identical to legacy thanks to SHA-256 associativity

MP-EXTRACT.NAME: Pure-Python content/style extraction port (A4 port)
MP-EXTRACT.purpose: Port of src/mint/extract.py to the pure-python tree. Preserves the legacy public API (extract_style / parse_theme / analyze_layouts / ExtractionFailedError / UnsupportedFormatError). Used by Operator workflows (UC-004 design-tokens baseline). OUTPUT SHAPE: flat dict {colors, typography, format, xml_sources, detected_layouts?} (preserves legacy shape under forbidden-3 NO-DIVERGENCE; the dev-plan abstract description {format, theme:{...}, layouts:[...]} is a higher-level summary — the wrap layer MP-MCP-EXTRACT will canonicalize the flat shape if a nested shape is desired). NAMESPACES dict + _detect_format duplicated inline from mint._xml_ns since constraint-8 only excepts mint._security.safe_doc.
MP-EXTRACT.depends: lxml ≥5.x, stdlib (zipfile, pathlib, logging). Constraint-8 compliant — 0 hits on `from mint\.|import mint\.` grep. NAMESPACES + _detect_format duplicated inline rather than imported from mint._xml_ns.
MP-EXTRACT.consumed-by: MP-MCP-EXTRACT (Phase-16 W2; wraps extract_style); future design-token baseline callers under mint_python/

fn-extract_style.PURPOSE: public entry; opens zip → parse_theme + analyze_layouts → flat dict; wraps everything except UnsupportedFormatError in ExtractionFailedError (matches legacy contract); missing theme.xml returns empty-theme dict WITHOUT raising (legacy parity)

fn-parse_theme.PURPOSE: theme.xml parser; lxml-driven; returns dict of color names + typography; empty dict on missing theme

fn-analyze_layouts.PURPOSE: layout enumerator; ECMA-376 walkthrough; returns list of layout summaries

fn-_detect_format.PURPOSE: private; .docx/.pptx suffix sniffer; raises UnsupportedFormatError on anything else

type-ExtractionFailedError.PURPOSE: exception; raised by extract_style on BadZipFile / missing-file / wraps lxml ParseError

type-UnsupportedFormatError.PURPOSE: exception; raised by _detect_format on .txt etc.; public for callers who want to distinguish from extraction failures

const-NAMESPACES.PURPOSE: dict of OOXML namespace prefix → URI; duplicated inline from mint._xml_ns per constraint-8

log-marker-BLOCK_EXTRACT_DONE.PURPOSE: INFO; payload format theme_keys_count layouts_count; NEW marker added during port (legacy had no equivalent)

MP-DOC-BUNDLE.NAME: Phase-16 doc_types bundle (report, ADR, contract, bilingual NDA)
MP-DOC-BUNDLE.purpose: Pure-data module shipping four new YAML doc_types. Consumed by the existing MP-DOC-GENERIC pipeline with zero Python changes. Bilingual NDA uses stacked Ru/En paragraphs (no engine extension). Bilingual recipe is a load-bearing invariant for future i18n doc_types: paired <field>_<lang> required_fields + adjacent paragraph blocks in `layout`. Demo coverage for O!Bank: product reports, product decisions (ADR), vendor contracts, cross-border NDAs.
MP-DOC-BUNDLE.depends: MP-DOC-GENERIC (consumer pipeline; relies on DocumentSpec being a non-frozen @dataclass so the 4 new templates' ~20 non-DocumentSpec fields are set via setattr — if DocumentSpec ever becomes frozen / __slots__-bound, this module breaks), MP-TEMPLATES-REGISTRY (schema validator).
MP-DOC-BUNDLE.consumed-by: create_document tool; any future doc-type catalog UI

yaml-report.PURPOSE: doc_type=report; required_fields=(title, author, date, summary, sections, conclusions); layout walks each section heading+body

yaml-decision-record.PURPOSE: doc_type=decision-record; required_fields=(title, author, date, status, context, decision, consequences); ADR shape

yaml-contract.PURPOSE: doc_type=contract; required_fields=(parties, effective_date, term, scope, obligations, signatures); formal contract shape

yaml-nda-bilingual.PURPOSE: doc_type=nda-bilingual-ru-en; required_fields=(party_a, party_b, effective_date, scope_ru, scope_en, term_ru, term_en, signatures); stacked Ru/En clause layout (each clause = paired paragraphs scope_ru followed by scope_en in body order)

pattern-bilingual-stacked.PURPOSE: Canonical recipe for bilingual doc_types: paired <field>_<lang> required_fields + adjacent paragraph blocks. Validated by V-MP-DOC-BUNDLE scenario-4. Reusable for NDA-Ky, Contract-Kk, etc. — zero engine work.

MP-MCP-FINGERPRINT.NAME: mint_fingerprint_document MCP tool (A3 wrap)
MP-MCP-FINGERPRINT.purpose: MCP wrap over MP-FINGERPRINT. Returns stable 5-key dict {hash, format, has_styles_xml, byte_count, drift_status}; drift_status is None (JSON null) when no baseline_hash supplied (short-circuits compare() rather than relying on UNKNOWN — keeps the wire contract clean). Useful for detecting accidental theme/style drift across model regenerations.
MP-MCP-FINGERPRINT.path: src/mint_python/mcp/fingerprint.py + module-tail deferred-import in src/mint_python/mcp/document.py to register @server.tool
MP-MCP-FINGERPRINT.depends: MP-FINGERPRINT (Phase-16 W1 port, commit b5d4748), mint._security.safe_doc, fastmcp.Context + ToolError
MP-MCP-FINGERPRINT.consumed-by: External MCP clients via mint_fingerprint_document tool

fn-mint_fingerprint_document.PURPOSE: @server.tool async fn(document_path, baseline_hash=None, *, ctx) → 5-key dict; surfaces InvalidDocument / MissingStylesXml as structured errors

fn-_canonicalize_result.PURPOSE: FingerprintResult + optional baseline_hash → 5-key dict; drift_status as StrEnum.value or None

log-marker-BLOCK_FP_DONE.PURPOSE: INFO; success path; payload hash format drift_status

log-marker-BLOCK_FP_MISSING_STYLES.PURPOSE: INFO; missing-styles raise path; payload document_path

MP-MCP-EXTRACT.NAME: mint_extract_content MCP tool (A4 wrap)
MP-MCP-EXTRACT.purpose: MCP wrap over MP-EXTRACT. **Shape decision (option B nested reshape)**: returns 4-key dict {format, theme: {colors, typography, xml_sources}, layouts: [...], extracted_at: ISO8601}. Rationale: W1 port preserves the flat dict for legacy parity (forbidden-3), but the MCP wrap is a new public surface with no existing client to preserve — the nested shape is what the dev-plan output spec called for and is semantically self-describing for LLM-as-client consumers. `extracted_at` is wrap-time provenance not in the pure extractor. Missing-theme docx returns success with empty theme (NOT EXTRACTION_FAILED — matches W1 legacy parity).
MP-MCP-EXTRACT.path: src/mint_python/mcp/extract.py + module-tail deferred-import in src/mint_python/mcp/document.py to register @server.tool
MP-MCP-EXTRACT.depends: MP-EXTRACT (Phase-16 W1 port, commit a020cfd), mint._security.safe_doc, fastmcp.Context + ToolError
MP-MCP-EXTRACT.consumed-by: External MCP clients via mint_extract_content tool

fn-mint_extract_content.PURPOSE: @server.tool async fn(document_path, *, ctx) → 4-key nested dict; surfaces UNSUPPORTED_FORMAT / EXTRACTION_FAILED / INVALID_DOCUMENT

fn-_reshape_tokens.PURPOSE: flat W1 dict {colors,typography,format,xml_sources,detected_layouts?} → nested {format, theme:{...}, layouts, extracted_at}

type-ExtractionFailed.PURPOSE: ExtractionFailedError surfaced as structured tool error

MP-THEME-EDIT.NAME: Structured preset editor MCP tools (palette/typography/spacing)
MP-THEME-EDIT.purpose: Three structured-patch MCP tools so a model can edit preset design tokens without composing valid YAML. Each tool validates a structured patch, merges into the current preset, writes a versioned `presets/<name>_v<next-semver>.yaml` (NEVER overwrite — concurrent-write race surfaces PRESET_VERSION_CONFLICT via OS-level O_EXCL). All gate through MP-AUTH-SHIM reusing MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS allowlist (themes are sub-noun of templates per VF-022 inv-2 + forbidden-4). Mirrors MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE versioning + audit-log pattern.
MP-THEME-EDIT.path: src/mint_python/mcp/preset_edit.py + module-tail deferred-import in src/mint_python/mcp/document.py to register 3 @server.tool decorators
MP-THEME-EDIT.depends: MP-AUTH-SHIM (`require_template_writer` + TemplateWriteForbidden + MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS allowlist), MP-STYLE (BUILTIN_PRESETS for base read; falls back to `presets/<name>_v<latest>.yaml` if prior edit exists), pyyaml, fastmcp.Context + ToolError. NOTE: MP-MCP-RESOURCES does NOT currently chain to versioned preset files — its `mint://preset/<name>` handler reads BUILTIN_PRESETS only. After a preset edit, the new versioned YAML is NOT discoverable via mint:// URIs until MP-MCP-RESOURCES is extended (candidate Phase-17 work).
MP-THEME-EDIT.consumed-by: Frontier-model agents on the writer allowlist; future preset-tuning workflows

fn-mint_update_preset_palette.PURPOSE: @server.tool async; merges palette dict (primary/secondary/accent/text/background hex strings); emits [MP-ThemeEdit][palette][BLOCK_PRESET_WRITE]

fn-_apply_palette_patch.PURPOSE: pure dict-merge + HEX_RE validation; returns list[str] of keys actually changed

fn-_apply_typography_patch.PURPOSE: pure dict-merge + range validation (positive sizes, non-empty fonts); returns list[str]

fn-_apply_spacing_patch.PURPOSE: pure dict-merge + non-negative validation; returns list[str]

fn-_bump_preset_version.PURPOSE: reads max version among `presets/<name>_v*.yaml` siblings; returns next semver minor bump; mirrors MP-TEMPLATES-REGISTRY._bump_template_version

fn-_resolve_base_preset_path.PURPOSE: scans PRESETS_DIR for latest `<name>_v*.yaml`; falls back to BUILTIN_PRESETS — chains edits cleanly so built-ins only resolve on first-touch

fn-_write_versioned_preset.PURPOSE: atomic write via os.open(..., O_CREAT|O_EXCL); collision raises PresetVersionConflict so concurrent writers serialize deterministically

fn-_append_preset_audit.PURPOSE: appends one JSONL line to presets/_audit.jsonl with keys {audit_id, timestamp, name, surface, version, predecessor_version, author, patched_fields, content_sha256, written_to}

type-InvalidPatch.PURPOSE: INVALID_PATCH structured error — raised BEFORE any disk I/O

type-PresetVersionConflict.PURPOSE: PRESET_VERSION_CONFLICT — raised by O_EXCL collision in concurrent-write race

const-PALETTE_KEYS.PURPOSE: frozenset of allowed palette patch keys

const-TYPOGRAPHY_KEYS.PURPOSE: frozenset of allowed typography patch keys

const-SPACING_KEYS.PURPOSE: frozenset of allowed spacing patch keys

const-HEX_RE.PURPOSE: hex color regex for palette validation

const-PRESETS_DIR.PURPOSE: module-level Path; production = src/mint_python/core/presets; tests monkeypatch to tmp_path

type-PatchFn.PURPOSE: Callable[[dict[str, Any], dict[str, Any]], list[str]] — type alias for the 3 patch functions

log-marker-BLOCK_PRESET_WRITE.PURPOSE: INFO per tool surface; payload name version patched_fields content_sha256; writers tuple NEVER leaked (forbidden-3 mirror)

MP-OOXML.NAME: Pure-Python OOXML pack/unpack port (W3a pre-port)
MP-OOXML.purpose: Scope-expansion 2026-05-11 after Wave-16-2 close. Pre-flight inventory of edit.py found 3 hot-path imports from mint.ooxml (unpack, pack, validate_relationships). mint.ooxml itself is fully self-contained (zero from-mint imports). Pre-ported BEFORE MP-EDIT to keep W3b's worker brief simple. Preserves the legacy public API verbatim — public symbol names + dataclass field names byte-identical to legacy (verified by TestForbiddenBehaviors.test_forbidden_3_public_symbol_names_match_legacy). Legacy module stays additively.
MP-OOXML.depends: stdlib only (logging, re, unicodedata, zipfile, dataclasses, pathlib, typing) + lxml ≥5.x. Constraint-8 compliant — zero `from mint.*` imports.
MP-OOXML.consumed-by: MP-EDIT (Phase-16 W3b; uses unpack + pack + validate_relationships in the edit pipeline); future pure-python callers needing OOXML pack/unpack. Reusable parity oracle at tests/_helpers/ooxml_parity.py::collect_parity(doc_path, work_dir) -> ParityRecord — runs unpack+pack on both implementations and returns a side-by-side record (manifests + auto-repair counters); W3b can call this to validate any edit pipeline path ending in a pack call.

fn-unpack.PURPOSE: fn-unpack(document_path, unpack_dir) -> UnpackResult — opens zip, pretty-prints XML parts, returns manifest enumerating files; binary parts byte-preserved; emits [MP-OOXML][unpack][BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK]

fn-pack.PURPOSE: fn-pack(unpack_dir, output_path) -> PackResult — repackages tree; runs auto-repair (durableId regeneration, xml:space=preserve insertion, rsid normalization); returns PackResult with _stats; emits [MP-OOXML][pack][BLOCK_OOXML_PACK]

fn-validate_relationships.PURPOSE: fn-validate_relationships(unpack_dir) -> None — raises OOXMLError on dangling relationships

fn-merge_runs.PURPOSE: fn-merge_runs(tree) -> int — coalesce adjacent w:r elements with identical run properties; returns merged count

fn-escape_smart_quotes.PURPOSE: fn-escape_smart_quotes(tree) -> int — Unicode smart quote → ASCII normalization on text nodes; returns mutation count

fn-_process_xml_for_pack.PURPOSE: private; merges runs + escapes smart quotes on each XML part before zipping

fn-_autorepair_durable_ids.PURPOSE: private; deterministic monotonic durableId regeneration with collision skip

fn-_autorepair_whitespace_preserve.PURPOSE: private; inserts xml:space=preserve where text has leading/trailing whitespace; emits [MP-OOXML][autorepair][BLOCK_OOXML_AUTOREPAIR]

fn-_validate_relationships.PURPOSE: private; internal helper backing the public validate_relationships

type-OOXMLError.PURPOSE: exception base with .code attribute; codes preserved verbatim from legacy: OOXML_NOT_A_ZIP, OOXML_MISSING_CONTENT_TYPES, OOXML_XML_PARSE_ERROR, OOXML_RELATIONSHIP_BROKEN, OOXML_PACK_FAILED, default OOXML_UNKNOWN

type-UnpackResult.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass: {unpack_dir, parts, runs_merged, quotes_escaped, format, original_path} — byte-identical fields to legacy

type-PackResult.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass: {output_path, repaired_durable_ids, preserved_whitespace_runs, bytes_written} — byte-identical fields to legacy

type-_PackStats.PURPOSE: private mutable counter dataclass used during pack; preserved verbatim from legacy

log-marker-BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK.PURPOSE: INFO; emitted on successful unpack; payload format parts_count

log-marker-BLOCK_OOXML_PACK.PURPOSE: INFO; emitted on successful pack; payload output_path bytes_written

log-marker-BLOCK_OOXML_AUTOREPAIR.PURPOSE: INFO; emitted when auto-repair mutates the tree (durableId regen / xml:space insertion); payload repair_kind count

caveat-pythondocx-relative-rels.PURPOSE: Known pre-existing issue (in legacy too, not a port divergence): python-docx-generated docx contains a relative rel Target='../customXml/item1.xml' that neither legacy nor port _resolve_target normalizes correctly. valid_memo_docx_bytes() therefore can't round-trip pack→unpack→pack; use minimal_docx_bytes() as the round-trip-safe fixture. Candidate Phase-17 follow-up: extend _resolve_target with .. normalization.

MP-EDIT.NAME: Pure-Python edit pipeline port (A5 port — biggest lift)
MP-EDIT.purpose: Port of src/mint/edit.py shipped at 1914 LOC — the largest single port of the Pure Python Edition cycle. Preserves the legacy M-EDIT public surface byte-identically. 151 tests cover all 12 V-MP-EDIT scenarios + coverage-tail branches; coverage 97% (23 lines uncovered are defensive `parent is None` branches and rare reparse paths). Porting-parity scenario-8 uses `pytest.importorskip("mint.edit")` so legacy can retire cleanly later. Legacy module stays additively.
MP-EDIT.depends: mint_python.ooxml (W3a port — unpack/pack/validate_relationships), mint_python.validate (W1 — run_checks + SeverityMode self-defined there), lxml ≥5.x, python-docx ≥1.1. MUST NOT import from src/mint/. NO mint.config dependency — SeverityMode comes from mint_python.validate; MintConfig is NOT imported (the only config field edit() reads is severity_mode, passed explicitly).
MP-EDIT.consumed-by: MP-MCP-EDIT (Phase-16 W3c; wraps edit + validate_plan + edit_plan_from_dict); future direct-Python edit-pipeline callers

fn-edit.PURPOSE: entry; signature `edit(document_path: str|Path, edit_plan: EditPlan, author: str='MINT', output_path: Path|None=None, severity_mode: SeverityMode|None=None) -> EditResult`; raises EditError on fail-fast (PPTX/invalid plan/backup failed/strict-mode regression/output==backup collision); returns EditResult(success=False, error=msg, output_path=None) on mid-pipeline op failure (pipeline aborts on first failed op per DF-007 step-4)

fn-edit_plan_from_dict.PURPOSE: fn-edit_plan_from_dict(raw: dict) -> EditPlan — JSON dict → EditPlan; raises EditError(code='EDIT_PLAN_INVALID') on shape errors; the natural seam for MCP wrap's JSON input

fn-validate_plan.PURPOSE: shape gate; raises EditError on (a) empty/oversize/ASCII-control-char anchor.value (catches NUL-injected OOXML sentinels), (b) unsupported op_type, (c) duplicate op_id, (d) revision ops missing author/date. Does NOT reject literal '<w:r>' substring — legacy parity. UC-008 acceptance is upheld via build_edit_prompt redaction (LLM input path) + control-char rejection; the MP-MCP-EDIT wrap (W3c) MAY add literal-substring rejection as a wrap-layer hardening step.

fn-build_edit_prompt.PURPOSE: builds LLM prompt for edit-flow callers; redacts < / > in anchor context so OOXML never reaches the LLM (UC-008 substantive guarantee)

fn-resolve_anchor.PURPOSE: resolver used by both LLM-driven and direct API paths; raises EditError(EDIT_ANCHOR_NOT_FOUND / EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS)

fn-_execute_op.PURPOSE: private; op-handler dispatch — text-replace, paragraph-insert/delete, retag, tracked-change, comment ops, accept/reject; mutates lxml tree in place; records OpOutcome

fn-apply_tracked_change.PURPOSE: emits ECMA-376 w:ins/w:del; routes tracked-change ops

fn-_CommentsState.PURPOSE: private state container; load/flush/next_comment_id/next_para_id/find_para_id_for_comment for comment subsystem

type-EditPlan.PURPOSE: dataclass {format: Literal['docx','pptx'], ops: list[EditOp], metadata: EditMetadata}

type-EditOp.PURPOSE: dataclass {type: str, op_id: str, anchor: Anchor, payload: dict[str, Any]}

type-OpOutcome.PURPOSE: dataclass {op_id, success, error_code, affected_part, before_snippet, after_snippet}

type-EditResult.PURPOSE: dataclass {output_path: Path|None, backup_path: Path, success: bool, ops_total: int, ops_succeeded: int, ops_failed: int, validation_report: Any, diff: list[OpOutcome], duration_ms: int, error: str|None}

type-EditError.PURPOSE: exception with .code attribute; codes preserved verbatim from legacy: EDIT_PLAN_INVALID, EDIT_OP_UNSUPPORTED, EDIT_ANCHOR_NOT_FOUND, EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS, EDIT_TRACKED_CHANGE_INVALID, EDIT_VALIDATION_FAILED, BACKUP_FAILED (NOT EDIT_BACKUP_FAILED — legacy spelling), EDIT_UNKNOWN

log-marker-BLOCK_EDIT_BACKUP.PURPOSE: INFO; payload backup_path; MUST fire before any BLOCK_EDIT_APPLY_OP (VF-021 inv-2)

log-marker-BLOCK_EDIT_EXTRACT_TEXT.PURPOSE: INFO; emitted during anchor extraction; payload anchors_count

log-marker-BLOCK_EDIT_RESOLVE_ANCHOR.PURPOSE: INFO per op; payload op_id miss?

log-marker-BLOCK_EDIT_APPLY_OP.PURPOSE: INFO per op; payload op_id op_type outcome

log-marker-BLOCK_EDIT_TRACKED_CHANGE.PURPOSE: INFO; emitted by apply_tracked_change for tracked ops

caveat-backup-failed-code.PURPOSE: Legacy and port both use error code `BACKUP_FAILED` (NOT `EDIT_BACKUP_FAILED` as V-MP-MCP-EDIT contract spells). MP-MCP-EDIT (W3c) wrap maps BACKUP_FAILED → EDIT_BACKUP_FAILED to match its own public contract.

caveat-uc008-rejection-at-wrap.PURPOSE: Worker brief expected validate_plan to reject literal '<w:r>' substring; legacy doesn't (only control-char + oversize + empty). UC-008 substantive guarantee preserved via build_edit_prompt redaction. If wrap-layer hard reject is desired, add it in MP-MCP-EDIT before calling edit().

MP-MCP-EDIT.NAME: mint_edit_document MCP tool (A5 wrap)
MP-MCP-EDIT.purpose: MCP wrap over MP-EDIT (W3b). Validates plan shape via edit_plan_from_dict; adds WRAP-LAYER hardening that rejects literal OOXML substrings (case-insensitive `<w:r` / `<w:p` / `<w:t` / `</w:`) in anchor.value BEFORE invoking edit() — satisfies V-MP-MCP-EDIT scenario-3 + VF-021 inv-3 NO-RAW-OOXML-IN-PLAN + UC-008 acceptance at the MCP boundary explicitly. Legacy MP-EDIT.validate_plan does NOT do this literal-substring reject; the substantive UC-008 guarantee at the core is build_edit_prompt redaction; this wrap adds the hard reject as a hardening layer. Mutates caller-supplied path; backup invariant inherited from MP-EDIT. NO allowlist (caller-owned doc).
MP-MCP-EDIT.path: src/mint_python/mcp/edit.py + module-tail deferred-import in src/mint_python/mcp/document.py to register @server.tool
MP-MCP-EDIT.depends: MP-EDIT (Phase-16 W3b port, commit 254f051), mint_python.validate.SeverityMode (W1 self-defined), mint._security.safe_doc, fastmcp.Context + ToolError. Reuses mint_python.mcp.validate._canonicalize_report for validation_report serialization (single source of truth — keeps shape parity with mint_validate_document).
MP-MCP-EDIT.consumed-by: External MCP clients via mint_edit_document tool

fn-mint_edit_document.PURPOSE: @server.tool async fn(document_path, plan_json, severity_mode='lenient', *, ctx) → 10-key canonical dict; surfaces 8 EditError variants as structured ToolError subclasses; emits BLOCK_EDIT_DONE outer marker after edit() returns

fn-_canonicalize_edit_result.PURPOSE: EditResult dataclass → 10-key dict (paths stringified; validation_report via _canonicalize_report; diff list of OpOutcome dicts)

fn-_reject_raw_ooxml_in_plan.PURPOSE: wrap-layer hardening; walks plan_json['ops'][*]['anchor']['value'] for case-insensitive substrings in _RAW_OOXML_NEEDLES; raises EditPlanInvalid naming the offending op_id BEFORE edit_plan_from_dict / edit() touches anything

fn-_remap_edit_error.PURPOSE: EditError.code → ToolError subclass dispatch via _EDIT_ERROR_MAP; unknown codes → EditUnknown (forward-compat for future MP-EDIT refactors)

fn-_synthesize_mid_pipeline_error.PURPOSE: when EditResult.success=False AND output_path is None AND diff non-empty, synthesizes EditError(code=diff[-1].error_code) with op_id=diff[-1].op_id and routes through _remap_edit_error — surfaces mid-pipeline op failures as structured ToolError rather than success envelopes

const-CANONICAL_EDIT_RESULT_KEYS.PURPOSE: ('output_path','backup_path','success','ops_total','ops_succeeded','ops_failed','validation_report','diff','duration_ms','error') — mirrors EditResult field order; VF-021 inv-6 STABLE-KEYS oracle

const-_RAW_OOXML_NEEDLES.PURPOSE: ('<w:r','<w:p','<w:t','</w:') case-insensitive — unambiguous OOXML run/paragraph/text markers; checked against anchor.value strings only

type-EditPlanInvalid.PURPOSE: EDIT_PLAN_INVALID from EditError OR wrap-layer raw-OOXML rejection

type-EditAnchorNotFound.PURPOSE: EDIT_ANCHOR_NOT_FOUND; carries op_id from EditResult.diff[-1].op_id on mid-pipeline failures

type-EditBackupFailed.PURPOSE: EDIT_BACKUP_FAILED at the wrap public contract; remaps from EditError.code='BACKUP_FAILED' (legacy spelling)

type-EditUnknown.PURPOSE: forward-compat catch-all for future EditError codes

log-marker-BLOCK_EDIT_DONE.PURPOSE: INFO; outer integration marker emitted by the wrap (MP-EDIT itself does not emit this); payload ops_total ops_succeeded ops_failed duration_ms

CrossLink.relation: mint_validate_document is a thin MCP wrap over mint_python.validate.validate — no re-implementation; canonicalizes ValidationReport to a stable-key-order dict.
mint_fix_document is a thin MCP wrap over mint_python.fix.fix; preserves the .bak-before-mutation invariant at the integration boundary.
mint_fingerprint_document wraps MP-FINGERPRINT.fingerprint + compare; the wrap CANNOT land until MP-FINGERPRINT port merges (W1 → W2 dependency).
mint_extract_content wraps MP-EXTRACT.extract_style; the wrap CANNOT land until MP-EXTRACT port merges (W1 → W2 dependency).
mint_edit_document wraps MP-EDIT.edit + validate_plan; CANNOT land until MP-EDIT port merges (W3 sequential).

The three preset-edit tools call require_template_writer BEFORE any disk mutation, reusing MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS allowlist. Themes are sub-noun of templates — NO separate MINT_PRESET_WRITERS env (VF-022 inv-2 + forbidden-4).
Each tool reads the current preset via MP-STYLE.load_preset(name=name) to merge a patch onto it. BUILTIN_PRESETS supplies the base; later versions resolve presets/<name>_v<X>.yaml.
The mint://preset/<name> resource handler from Phase-14 surfaces the new versioned files without code changes; structured edits feed back into the resource graph cleanly.

Phase-16 preset writes mirror the Phase-14 W3 template-write pattern: versioned YAML, never overwrite, _audit.jsonl append, GRACE manifest lineage. Future Phase-17 could unify into a single MP-GOVERNED-WRITE module.
Pure-data: the 4 new YAML doc_types are consumed by the existing create_document pipeline. Zero Python changes — Phase-14's "adding a doc_type is a YAML file" promise lands in practice.
Each new template loads through TemplateRegistry's schema validator; malformed YAMLs are caught at registry load, before any create_document call.
MP-VALIDATE consumes fingerprint hashes for structural-drift signals; the Phase-16 port preserves the legacy API so this consumer needs no change.
Post-pack re-validation; preserves the legacy DF-007 pipeline pattern with no consumer change.
Drift check after edit; uses MP-FINGERPRINT (the Phase-16 port) so the constraint-8 NO-LEGACY-IMPORT gate holds.

Phase-16 W3b depends on W3a port. The 3 hot-path operations in edit (unpack input docx → resolve anchors → apply ops → pack output) call mint_python.ooxml.unpack / .validate_relationships / .pack respectively. Scope-expansion 2026-05-11 pre-ported MP-OOXML to keep this dependency inside the pure-python tree.

MP-DOC-PERSONAL-GUARD.NAME: Privacy mitigations for create_document
MP-DOC-PERSONAL-GUARD.purpose: Mitigates the Phase-16 production gap: Claude Desktop chat-driven elicit-fallback fills `sender` from session context without prompting the user. MINT can MITIGATE via intent-flag detection + heuristic blocklist + anonymisation reporting + walker conditional-skip + elicit-skip for blocklisted-and-optional fields; cannot ENFORCE (client decides what to fill in elicit responses). Story: "MINT respects anonymity hints". forbidden-9 mechanically enforced via runtime grep test (test_forbidden_9_no_enforcement_claims_in_source).
MP-DOC-PERSONAL-GUARD.path: src/mint_python/mcp/document.py (+369 LOC: _heuristic_extract wrapper, _run_pipeline structured_content shape, _build_document walker conditional-skip via _prepare_layout, _audit_instructions extension) + templates/letter.yaml (sender → optional)
MP-DOC-PERSONAL-GUARD.depends: existing MP-DOC-GENERIC heuristic extractor + walker + audit-instructions builder; existing templates/letter.yaml; stdlib `hashlib` for value_sha8 redaction; no new external deps
MP-DOC-PERSONAL-GUARD.consumed-by: external MCP clients via mint_create_document tool when intent contains anonymity-hint phrases; mint_create_memo (alias) inherits the behavior

fn-_detect_anonymous_flag.PURPOSE: anchored named-group regex over intent → `tuple[bool, str | None]` (matched, form). Form is one of {bracket, word_ru, phrase_ru} (English bare `anonymous` is too ambiguous — only the bracket form fires for EN). Pure regex; no I/O.

fn-_apply_personal_blocklist.PURPOSE: clears _PERSONAL_FIELDS values in DocumentSpec; returns (modified_spec, sorted_cleared_list); emits BLOCK_ANONYMISE per cleared field with value_sha8 + value_len redaction (raw value NEVER logged — forbidden-6)

fn-_render_anonymisation_report.PURPOSE: merges anonymised:bool + fields_omitted:list[str] into tool result structured_content alongside existing keys

fn-_extract_with_anonymisation.PURPOSE: wrapper preserving the legacy `_heuristic_extract(intent, source_md) -> DocumentSpec` signature MP-Memo tests depend on; threads anonymisation state through

fn-_prepare_layout.PURPOSE: 2-pass walker pre-processor (~50 LOC): Phase A drops placeholder-only-empty blocks; Phase B drops decorative headings whose immediate non-spacer body block was dropped. Scope-expanded from V-MP-DOC-PERSONAL-GUARD scenario-16.

fn-_entry_resolves_empty.PURPOSE: Phase-A helper: returns True for kind ∈ {paragraph, heading} where text matches placeholder-only regex AND spec.field is empty AND no `default:` YAML key

type-_AnonymisationOutcome.PURPOSE: dataclass threading anonymised flag + fields_omitted list through the pipeline without leaking values

const-_PERSONAL_FIELDS.PURPOSE: 15-name frozenset: sender, author, contact, from_, to_, signature, signer, recipient, cc, bcc, phone, email, address, signer_name, signer_email

const-_ANONYMOUS_FLAG_RE.PURPOSE: named-group regex with bracket / word_ru / phrase_ru alternatives; anchored (no-word lookbehind/lookahead for Cyrillic word form); IGNORECASE

const-_PLACEHOLDER_ONLY_RE.PURPOSE: regex matching layout text containing exactly one `{{ field }}` placeholder with optional surrounding whitespace

log-marker-BLOCK_ANONYMOUS_DETECTED.PURPOSE: INFO; payload match_form={bracket|word_ru|phrase_ru} intent_len=<int>; once per call when flag detected

log-marker-BLOCK_ANONYMISE.PURPOSE: INFO; payload field=<name> reason=anonymous_intent_flag value_len=<int> value_sha8=<hex8>; once per cleared field; raw value NEVER logged

log-marker-BLOCK_PERSONAL_ELICIT_HINT.PURPOSE: INFO; payload field=<name>; once per personal-field elicit prompt that includes the personal_data=high hint

MP-DOC-TECH-SPEC.NAME: Phase-17 technical-spec doc_type (demonstrates kind:table + kind:callout)
MP-DOC-TECH-SPEC.purpose: Closes catalog gap from Phase-16 production test (real-world TZ request → MINT picked letter). Pure-data: templates/technical-spec.yaml using the FULL engine layout vocabulary (heading + paragraph + table + callout + spacer). **KEY DISCOVERY**: walker has supported table + callout since Phase-14 — V-MP-DOC-BUNDLE forbidden-1 mis-froze them. This module amends that constraint + demonstrates the existing capability via a real doc_type. Engineering docs (CSV schemas, calibration data, technical specs) get a first-class shape.
MP-DOC-TECH-SPEC.depends: MP-DOC-GENERIC (consumer pipeline — walker already supports table + callout), MP-TEMPLATES-REGISTRY (schema validator), mint_python.core.table.Table (Phase-7; consumed by walker)
MP-DOC-TECH-SPEC.consumed-by: create_document tool when doc_type='technical-spec'; future engineering-doc workflows

yaml-technical-spec.PURPOSE: doc_type=technical-spec; required_fields=(title, purpose, sections); layout uses heading + paragraph + table + callout + spacer; sender/contact NOT in required_fields so anonymous calls work without elicit

pattern-engine-full-vocab.PURPOSE: canonical reference for using all 5 kinds in one template — reusable recipe for future engineering doc_types

MP-DOC-PICKER.NAME: Template suggestion + improved no-match UX (mint_suggest_template tool)
MP-DOC-PICKER.purpose: Closes UX gap from Phase-16 production test (silent template fallback). New tool `mint_suggest_template(intent, ctx)` returns top-3 keyword-scored alternatives with `why` reasoning, so LLM caller can ask BEFORE committing to doc_type. Improved DocumentTypeNotFound error surfaces same suggestions inline. Story: "MINT helps Claude pick well — but Claude still picks".
MP-DOC-PICKER.path: src/mint_python/mcp/picker.py + edit to DocumentTypeNotFound.__init__ in src/mint_python/mcp/document.py
MP-DOC-PICKER.depends: MP-TEMPLATES-REGISTRY (lists available templates + metadata), fastmcp.Context + ToolError
MP-DOC-PICKER.consumed-by: External MCP clients via mint_suggest_template tool; improved DocumentTypeNotFound error consumed by callers of mint_create_document

fn-mint_suggest_template.PURPOSE: @server.tool async fn(intent, ctx) → {suggestions: [...], total_templates: int}; non-failing (empty list when no templates exist)

fn-_score_templates.PURPOSE: keyword-overlap scorer; case-insensitive Jaccard-style intersection + _KEYWORD_BONUS_TERMS dictionary bumps

const-_KEYWORD_BONUS_TERMS.PURPOSE: dict mapping doc_type → strong keywords (e.g. technical-spec → 'тз','tz','technical','spec','requirements','техническое задание')

type-ScoredTemplate.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass: name str, match_score float, why str

log-marker-BLOCK_SUGGEST.PURPOSE: INFO; payload total_templates + top + score on every call

MP-AUDIT-EXTEND.NAME: GRACE manifest extended provenance (preset_version + lang metadata)
MP-AUDIT-EXTEND.purpose: Closes 2 Phase-16 deferrals sharing a single root cause: _audit_instructions stamps template_version but NOT preset_version (V-MP-THEME-EDIT scenario-10) nor lang metadata for bilingual templates (V-MP-DOC-BUNDLE scenario-7b). Phase-17 stamps both. After this lands the bilingual NDA cross-model handoff demo + preset-edit feedback loop work end-to-end.
MP-AUDIT-EXTEND.depends: MP-DOC-GENERIC (extends _audit_instructions build), MP-THEME-EDIT (versioned preset filename pattern), MP-MANIFEST-READ (downstream consumer surfaces the new fields)
MP-AUDIT-EXTEND.consumed-by: MP-GRACE.bootstrap (gets the extended instructions list); downstream mint_read_grace_manifest callers

fn-_resolve_active_preset_version.PURPOSE: scans presets/<name>_v*.yaml + falls back to BUILTIN_PRESETS base; defensive on missing files

fn-_detect_template_languages.PURPOSE: scans required_fields for `<base>_<lang>` pattern; returns sorted unique lang codes when ≥2 distinct codes appear

fn-_extend_audit_instructions.PURPOSE: appends `preset_version=...` always + `lang=...` when applicable to instructions list

log-marker-BLOCK_AUDIT_PRESET_VERSION.PURPOSE: INFO; payload preset=<name> version=<...>; fires on every successful create_document

log-marker-BLOCK_AUDIT_LANG.PURPOSE: INFO; payload codes=<list>; fires only when template has ≥2 distinct language-paired fields

MP-MCP-RESOURCES-VERSIONED.NAME: Versioned-preset chain in MCP resources
MP-MCP-RESOURCES-VERSIONED.purpose: Closes the 3rd Phase-16 gap surfaced at W2 review: existing mint://preset/<name> handler reads BUILTIN_PRESETS only, doesn't chain to versioned preset files written by mint_update_preset_*. After Phase-17 the preset-edit feedback loop is visible end-to-end via MCP resources; external clients (Cursor, OpenWebUI) see the latest version of each preset.
MP-MCP-RESOURCES-VERSIONED.path: src/mint_python/mcp/resources.py (resolver helpers + edits to existing handlers)
MP-MCP-RESOURCES-VERSIONED.depends: MP-MCP-RESOURCES (extends existing _preset_resource_content + list_presets + get_preset), MP-THEME-EDIT (consumes the PRESETS_DIR convention + versioned filename pattern defined there)
MP-MCP-RESOURCES-VERSIONED.consumed-by: External MCP clients reading mint://preset/<name> resource URI OR invoking mint_list_presets / mint_get_preset tools

fn-_resolve_latest_preset_path.PURPOSE: scans PRESETS_DIR for `<name>_v*.yaml`; semver sort (NOT lexicographic — '1.10' > '1.2'); falls back to BUILTIN_PRESETS on first-touch

fn-_collect_preset_versions.PURPOSE: returns sorted list of all version strings incl. base '1.0' for metadata reporting

log-marker-BLOCK_RESOLVE_VERSIONED.PURPOSE: INFO; payload name + resolved + predecessors_count on every preset read

CrossLink.relation: Edits document.py's _heuristic_extract + _run_pipeline structured_content shape. The new fields anonymised + fields_omitted appear alongside existing keys (path, audit_id, fields_elicited, doc_type, template_version, visual_qa).

Pure data; templates/technical-spec.yaml consumed by the existing create_document pipeline. Demonstrates that the layout walker has supported kind:table + kind:callout since Phase-14 — V-MP-DOC-BUNDLE forbidden-1 (Phase-16) mis-scoped this as engine-wide; Phase-17 amends.
The kind:table walker branch in document.py:531 calls mint_python.core.table.Table.from_list(...) — the existing Phase-7 SDK table model is what renders the tabular content of technical-spec.yaml.
Loads available templates + metadata via list_summaries; the picker is a read-only consumer of the registry's existing data.
Single edit to DocumentTypeNotFound.__init__ adds suggestion list to the structured error message. Otherwise picker.py is standalone.
Extends the _audit_instructions helper in document.py — the same helper that already stamps template_version. Single-owner edit; W17-3 sequential after W17-1 + W17-2 close.

Closes V-MP-THEME-EDIT scenario-10: the preset_version stamping is what makes the cross-model preset-edit demo visible in produced docx's GRACE manifest. **Shares the `resolve_latest_preset_path` helper** with MP-MCP-RESOURCES-VERSIONED — both import from `mint_python.mcp.preset_edit` after W17-0 controller pre-flight promoted the helper from private to public. Single source of truth for semver-tuple version resolution; drift between modules would silently regress to lex-sort.
Closes V-MP-DOC-BUNDLE scenario-7b: the lang metadata stamping makes bilingual NDA's language pairing visible in GRACE manifest — completes the bilingual cross-model handoff story.
mint_read_grace_manifest surfaces the extended instructions list verbatim via _canonicalize — callers parse `preset_version=...` and `lang=...` from the dict's instructions field.

Extends _preset_resource_content + list_presets + get_preset handlers to use _resolve_latest_preset_path instead of reading BUILTIN_PRESETS directly. After Phase-17, the resource URI surface chains to versioned files automatically.

Reads the versioned preset files MP-THEME-EDIT writes (presets/<name>_v<X>.yaml). The two modules close the preset-write-and-read loop together — write side in Phase-16 W2, read side in Phase-17 W2. **Imports public `resolve_latest_preset_path` from preset_edit.py** (promoted from private in W17-0 controller pre-flight) — same helper that MP-AUDIT-EXTEND imports, ensures both reader paths agree on what 'latest version' means. **Circular-import note (W17-2 discovery)**: preset_edit → mcp.document → mcp.resources → preset_edit forms a cycle at module-load if resources.py imports preset_edit at the top. Solution implemented in `_resolve_preset_for_read`: lazy import preset_edit inside the function. Future modules under mcp/ that need preset_edit helpers MUST follow this pattern.
